stack: client-side web app (static HTML/JS served by python-stdlib)

vulns.desc: DOM-based XSS: /search.html reads user-controlled location.hash (and location.search 'q') and writes it into the page via element.innerHTML and document.write with no encoding, so a crafted fragment/param executes script in the victim's browser.
vulns.exploit: Open /search.html# (or /search.html?q=

vulns.desc: Client-side prototype pollution: app.js merge() recursively copies attacker-controlled JSON into a target object with no __proto__/constructor guard, and /profile.html feeds it the URL ?prefs= value. A payload with a __proto__ key pollutes Object.prototype globally, enabling property injection / privilege-flag gadgets / DOM-XSS gadget chains.
vulns.exploit: GET /profile.html?prefs={"__proto__":{"isAdmin":true}} -> merge() walks into __proto__ -> every object now has isAdmin=true (Object.prototype polluted).

vulns.desc: DOM-based open redirect: /go.html sets location.href to the unvalidated ?next= parameter (no allow-list / same-origin check), so it redirects to arbitrary external URLs and even executes javascript: URIs.
vulns.exploit: GET /go.html?next=https://evil.example/ (or next=javascript:alert(1)) -> browser navigates to the attacker target.

vulns.desc: Secret in client-side code: app.js hardcodes a privileged INTERNAL_API_KEY (and an analytics token) that is shipped to and downloadable by every visitor.
vulns.exploit: GET /static/app.js -> INTERNAL_API_KEY = 'cf_live_...' visible in source, usable from any client.

vulns.desc: Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy (which would mitigate the DOM XSS), no X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, no X-Content-Type-Options.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any page -> no CSP/XFO/XCTO present.
